findings.issue: Task 16 has `wave: 8` and `depends_on: [15]`. Per validator rule, a task with depends_on:[X] must have wave > wave of task X. If Task 15 is also in wave 8 (likely — tech-spec groups Tasks 15/16/17 in 'Final Wave'), then the wave numbers don't reflect the dependency chain. Cross-task validation should confirm Task 15's wave; if Task 15 is also wave 8, this is a dependency-cycle/wave-ordering violation.
findings.fix: Either (a) assign distinct waves to 15/16/17 (e.g., 15→wave8, 16→wave9, 17→wave10) so depends_on relationships are consistent with wave ordering, or (b) confirm the team convention that sequential tasks within a 'Final Wave' can share a wave number — document this convention in tech-spec and update the validator's dependency rule. As written, the wave numbers are inconsistent with the dependency chain.

findings.issue: Task 17 has `wave: 8` and `depends_on: [16]`, but Task 16 is also `wave: 8`. Same wave-vs-dependency conflict as Task 16. Per the rule 'Task with depends_on:[X] must have wave > wave of task X', this is a violation.
findings.fix: Assign Task 17 a higher wave than Task 16 (e.g., wave: 9 or 10) to reflect the sequential dependency, or document and adopt the 'Final Wave shares wave number' convention explicitly.

findings.issue: Task 16 declares `deploy-reviewer` as a reviewer. This is a non-standard reviewer (not among the standard `code-reviewer`, `security-auditor`, `test-reviewer`, or other common reviewers). The validator could not confirm that a `deploy-reviewer` agent exists in `~/.claude/agents/`. If it doesn't exist, the task will fail at execution time when feature-execution attempts to spawn the reviewer.
findings.fix: Confirm `deploy-reviewer` agent exists at `~/.claude/agents/deploy-reviewer.md`. If not, either (a) create the agent definition first, (b) substitute an existing reviewer (e.g., `infrastructure-reviewer` if defined), or (c) drop to `code-reviewer` + `security-auditor` only and have a human do the deploy review.

findings.issue: Context Files contains a non-link entry: `contracts/deploy/` output — deploy log Task 16 ...`. This is plain text, not a markdown link `[name](path)`. Template requires all files as markdown links.
findings.fix: Replace with `[contracts/deploy/](../../../contracts/deploy/)` — deploy log Task 16 ...` (or link to a specific expected file in `contracts/deploy/` such as the deploy artifacts JSON).

findings.issue: Reviewer report paths use absolute filesystem paths (`/Users/syi/src/universal-paywall/work/...`) rather than the repository-relative paths in the template (`work/{feature}/logs/working/task-{N}/...`). Absolute paths break portability (only works on syi's machine) and don't match the template's convention.
findings.fix: Convert to relative paths: `work/x402-agent-payment/logs/working/task-16/code-reviewer-{round}.json` (and analogous for security-auditor and deploy-reviewer).

findings.issue: Reviewers section contains a prose paragraph explaining the absence of reviewers rather than a list. While the explanation is helpful and consistent with `reviewers: []` in frontmatter, the template's intent is that this section enumerates reviewer→report-path mappings. Empty reviewer list is acceptable per validator's reviewers rules (`Can be empty [] or contain none for self-verifying tasks (QA, deploy)`), so the prose explanation is a reasonable accommodation — minor stylistic deviation.
findings.fix: Optional: keep prose explanation but also add an explicit `- none` list item or a one-line note `(self-verifying — see post-deploy-report.md)` to match the list format used elsewhere. No structural fix strictly required.

findings.issue: The Smoke section contains an unresolved placeholder in the curl command: `http://localhost:<port>/protected` — `<port>` is a template variable, not a concrete port. While the surrounding prose explains that port is determined at runtime by the scratch server, the literal `<port>` placeholder is unusual in a Verification Steps block.
findings.fix: Either bind the server to a fixed test port (e.g., 8787) and use that literal in the curl, or rewrite the block to make explicit that the port is captured from server.js output (e.g., `PORT=$(node -e ...) && curl ... http://localhost:$PORT/...`). Same applies to the `node server.js` reference — clarify how the server.js content is constructed (`What to do` shows it but Verification Steps references a file that scratch dir doesn't yet have).

findings.issue: Tech-spec Implementation Tasks for Task 17 says tools are `bash, curl, viem (programmatic, not MCP)`, and references `factory.feeBps()` etc. The Context Files block references `networks.ts` and `contracts/deploy/` but does not list the contract ABI source (`contracts/contracts/PaymentSplitterFactory.sol`) or the e2e test file (`packages/middleware/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts`) that step 2 (live e2e) depends on. These are needed for the agent to know the factory ABI and confirm the e2e test exists.
findings.fix: Add to Context Files: `[PaymentSplitterFactory.sol](../../../contracts/contracts/PaymentSplitterFactory.sol)` — factory ABI source for on-chain read script; `[arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts](../../../packages/middleware/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts)` — live e2e test (from Task 10) executed via `npm run test:e2e`.

findings.section: Details / What to do — e2e file path
findings.issue: Task 17 says the e2e test was created in Task 11, but the tech-spec shows the live Arc Testnet e2e test belongs to Task 10 (`packages/middleware/__tests__/integration/arc-testnet-e2e.test.ts` in Wave 6, Task 10). Task 11 is the deploy script + register CLI + README. The `Dependencies` block in Task 17 attributes the e2e gate to Task 11.
findings.fix: Correct the dependency reference: change 'Task 11 (test suite) — `npm run test:e2e` с `ARC_TESTNET_E2E` гейтом написан там' to 'Task 10 (test suite) — `npm run test:e2e` с `ARC_TESTNET_E2E` гейтом написан там'. Same for the `What to do` mention of 'тест существует с Task 11'.

findings.issue: Task 16 mentions `contracts/deploy/artifacts/arcTestnet/` as a commit target but this artifact directory is not listed anywhere in tech-spec's Architecture > 'What we're building/modifying' or in Task 11's deliverables. Tech-spec only shows `contracts/deploy/01_deploy_factory.ts`. The task assumes Task 11 standardized the artifact output location but acknowledges uncertainty ('committed under `contracts/deploy/artifacts/arcTestnet/` (or wherever Task 11 standardized)').
findings.fix: Either (a) pin the artifact location explicitly in Task 11's tech-spec entry so Task 16 doesn't have to guess, or (b) accept that Task 16 will discover the location and document it as a follow-up. Currently the language ('or wherever Task 11 standardized') reads as a known gap.

findings.issue: Task 16 bundles two distinct deliverables: (a) on-chain deploy + arcscan verification + networks.ts patch, and (b) npm publish (build + dry-run + version bump + publish + tag). The task argues atomicity ('contracts and npm must land atomically'), but in practice these are independent operational steps with different failure modes (chain RPC vs npm registry auth/2FA). The Edge cases section even acknowledges they can fail independently ('npm publish fails after deploy succeeded ... 0.1.0-alpha.0 may be burned'). This is more than 1-3 files of scope.
findings.fix: Consider splitting into Task 16a (Deploy + verify + commit networks.ts) and Task 16b (npm publish + tag), where 16b depends_on 16a. This makes failure recovery cleaner (a publish-only retry doesn't require a re-deploy). However, the task-creator may have intentionally bundled them for atomicity — accept the bundling but acknowledge the deviation from atomicity ideal in tech-spec.
